pilots.name: CAAB Feasibility Pilot
pilots.description: Test absorption detection pipeline on 2 architectures (JumpReLU vs TopK) with first-letter features
pilots.objective: Verify that absorption rate differs significantly across architectures and the pipeline completes within 15 minutes
steps: Load GPT-2 Small via TransformerLens
Load pre-trained JumpReLU SAE for layer 8 (or train small TopK if unavailable)
Train small TopK SAE (width 4k, k=50) on 1k OpenWebText samples
Run Chanin et al. absorption detection on first-letter features (a-z)
Compute absorption rate for both architectures
Compare and verify >10% difference
pass_criteria: Absorption rate difference between architectures > 10 percentage points
pilots.fail_action: If JumpReLU SAE unavailable, train both architectures from scratch with reduced width (2k). If still failing, mark CAAB as high-risk and focus on single-architecture analysis.

pilots.name: Causal Evaluation Feasibility Pilot
pilots.description: Test sparse probing pipeline with high vs low absorption SAEs
pilots.objective: Verify that absorption rate correlates with probe accuracy and the pipeline completes within 15 minutes
steps: Train two TopK SAEs on GPT-2 Small layer 8: k=25 (high absorption) and k=100 (low absorption)
Define 20 concepts (4 per domain: animals, colors, numbers, countries)
Generate positive/negative examples for each concept
Train linear probes on SAE features for each concept
Compare probe AUROC between high and low absorption SAEs
pass_criteria: Probe accuracy difference > 5 percentage points between high and low absorption
pilots.fail_action: If no accuracy difference, try more concepts (50) or different concept types. If still no difference, note that H2 may be falsified.

pilots.name: UAD Feasibility Pilot
pilots.description: Test co-occurrence-based unsupervised detection on small feature set
pilots.objective: Verify that unsupervised methods can detect absorbed pairs with reasonable precision
steps: Load pre-trained SAE (GemmaScope or GPT-2)
Select 100 features (first-letter features + random sample)
Build activation co-occurrence matrix on 1k tokens
Compute mutual information and exclusivity scores for each pair
Compare top-scoring pairs with Chanin et al. supervised labels
Calculate precision and recall
pilots.fail_action: If precision/recall too low, try alternative scoring functions (reconstruction redundancy, activation ratio). If still failing, mark UAD as infeasible for this iteration.

pilots.name: DFDA Feasibility Pilot
pilots.description: Test residual compensation network on small number of absorbed pairs
pilots.objective: Verify that a small MLP can recover absorbed parent feature activations
steps: Identify 10 known absorbed pairs from Chanin et al. method
Build training dataset: (child_activation, parent_activation) pairs
Train small MLP: input=child feature, output=parent feature prediction
Evaluate: probe accuracy on parent concept before/after compensation
Measure reconstruction error change
pass_criteria: Probe accuracy improvement > 5% OR reconstruction error increase < 10%
pilots.fail_action: If no improvement, try larger MLP or different loss function. If compensation hurts reconstruction, mark DFDA as infeasible.

pilot_decision_matrix.p1_p2_pass_only: Proceed with core experiments (F1-F4), skip exploratory (F5-F6)
pilot_decision_matrix.p1_pass_only: Proceed with F1, F3, F4 (architecture comparison + trends). Skip causal and exploratory.
pilot_decision_matrix.p2_pass_only: Proceed with F2 only (causal analysis on limited SAE set). Consider training more architectures.
pilot_decision_matrix.none_pass: REFINE: Revisit methodology. Consider simpler detection metrics, smaller models, or alternative research questions.
